clean_review_text: A few years ago, I was trying to decide if I could wear contacts after 30 years of just wearing glasses. I struggled through many different pairs and brands of contacts and they all just never felt right. My eyes just felt gritty and dry. My doctor finally just made this comment, and it is very true: At a certain age women just have less moisture in their bodies (in this case it was my eyes). That phrase also applies to our skin, and of course the most visible is our face. This moisturizer is for those of us that need that extra little boost, as we might have "advanced" skin. Now in my late 40's I am noticing those fine lines, especially on my forehead, what I would call my furrowed brow lines. Will this moisturizer erase these lines? No. But it can help diminish appearance. An added benefit of this moisturizer is it also provides a moisture barrier to protect your skin from the elements. As a child of the 80's, we all grew up loving the sun and getting tan. Now we know better and with better products are able to protect our skin from the elements.

product_title: KORRES Santorini Grape Velvet Skin Drink, 1 fl. oz.
clean_review_title: Beautiful Oil For A Splurge- But Not Worth The Hype
clean_review_text: This is a beautiful oil to treat yourself, but the bottle is misleading. If you look carefully, you can see that the liquid does not reach the bottom, that there is another glass bottom a ways up, making the bottle appear more full than it is. I took a star off for that because that's just not cool. They could make it in a smaller bottle & people would still pay $48 for it. It's a nice product. I wish, however, that it didn't have the olive oil in it, because that's what I think is causing acne in a lot of girls skin. For those with acne or if you just don't want to pay this much, you can get the same "velvet" results with rosehip oil. The Ordinary has one for 10 bucks. Rosehip oil can help reduce your inflammatory acne, get rid of acne scars, and clear your skin. Rosehip oil is an anti-inflammatory, able to reduce swelling and fluid buildup. Rosehip oil has a low rating of 1-2 on the comedogenic scale (a.k.a. not likely to clog dem pores). Plus, the linoleic fatty acids in rosehip oil have been shown to reduce oil production in oily skin types which can help prevent future acne. I received this Velvet Skin Drink as a sample & like others, I was impressed, but I found it for brand new & sealed for $24 on Mercari. Personally, because of the olive oil filler and the bottle deception, I wasn't willing to pay $48. They do some great marketing, evidently, as it's out of stock all the time. They make it sound like it's made of purely grapes, which it's not & the bottle deception just isn't worth the hype. Yes, the product is beautiful & makes your skin very soft, but is it softer than my Ordinary Rosehip oil? Nah. This is something nice to splurge on if I find a good deal, because of the of Sarantoni grapes, Resveratrol and lactic acid. But there are no studies on the benefits of these grapes over other grapes & they don't say how much Resvertal and lactic acid they use. I wouldn't consider this an active of any of those products. The Ordinary has both for cheap.

clean_review_title: Only deodorant that works for me.
clean_review_text: Love this. This is the only deodorant I've found in all my life (I'm only 27 though) that works. Before this, deodorants did not work for me. My natural scent and the fake fragrance makes me smell horrible and would accentuate any BO I might have. My best bet was to go deodorant-less and just wash my armpits when they got stinky. Then I started using natural salt products. Those irritated my skin due to the dryness (so I basically had itchy armpits). Then I came across a coupon for Soft & Dri, it would come out to $2 so I figured what the heck, I'll try something new. Well it works. I'm still surprised it works. It's been three months and I think I still have half a can left. I only spray it very shortly. I don't spray it enough to leave any white residue. Do be careful when putting on clothes right after spraying it. Also, the scent is kind of strong - a powdery smell. It's not the kind of scent I'd prefer, but at this point, I don't mind smelling a little grandma-y when no deodorant has ever worked for me.

product_title: Tree of Life Vitamin C Facial Toner for Minimizing Pores | Toner and Makeup Remover for Face, 4 Fl oz
clean_review_text: First, I want to say I bought this at full price....period! I've been very lax over years, at any kind of skin care routine. My skin isn't really showing any effects of bad skin care, because I was blessed with really good skin, but....I'm 52 now and decided I really shouldn't push it any longer. So; I purchased Tree of Life Beauty Vitamin C wash first. I was very impressed with the ingredient list, the amount used to wash my face (neck, chest, and shoulders as well), and the way my skin felt afterwards. So when I saw the toner was 93% organic, I decided buy the Tree of life Beauty Toner, to go along with the wash. First of all, I really am impressed with the ingredients this company uses. I grow all organics herbs, berries, vegetables, etc. and use as many organic ingredients that I can. I would love to be a purist, but my budget says "NO!" This company doesn't use a lot of needless filler ingredients to stretch the product. So when you think of the product being only 8 oz, you can be assured that 8 oz (when used properly), is a good amount of product that will last. I can't say how long yet, as I've only been using it a week. Secondly, being an herbalist has taught me that your skin is your largest organ! You should be careful what you put on it. Your pores soak up every last drop of whatever you put on your skin. Parabens are absolute poison and should be avoided like the plague! (Sorry all of you Vicks and vasaline lovers out there) From what I've read about Tree of life Beauty, we're on the same page and I don't feel I need to be concerned about them hiding any in their products. And finally, Vitamin C is essential to your body in too many ways to go into. But I take a vitamin C daily to help with the absorption of Collagen 1&3 (Which is important for hair, skin, nails, bones, joints, etc). I had not realized the benefits of vitamin C for your face. I absolutely love the way my skin feels after using the cleanser and toner. I let it dry well, then follow up with moisturizer both morning and evening. So far, I'm quite happy with Tree of Life Beauty products and will probably transition to more of their products as I use up what I already have. We'll see how it goes after using these items for 30 days.

product_title: Ilishop Women's New Fashion Handbag Genuine Leather Shoulder Bags Tote Bags Hot Sale (Winered-small)
clean_review_title: Great for everyday or work use
clean_review_text: [[VIDEOID:ed7d61f010f8777fbaeea9b7323618aa]] I received this tote from the manufacturer at no cost for evaluation, and here are my thoughts. This small size wine red leather tote has a clean professional appearance that looks good enough to carry for everyday use or to work. It's large enough to fit everything I need on a daily basis without being overly big, and the handles are long enough to fit comfortably over my shoulder. It also comes with a detachable shoulder strap that adjusts long enough to carry it across a shoulder or cross body as well. It has a mild aroma when it first arrives, but that goes away in a day or two. This is definitely made of leather, but it's not thick full grain leather like you would get with an expensive bag like Coach. Of course, this doesn't cost what those purses do either. This tote is made of split leather that's polished to a smooth finish then bonded to a thicker substrate material and given a protective coating to make it soft and pliable while also being scratch and water resistant. It won't last as long as a full grain leather purse would, but it's well made and should last more than long enough to get your money's worth from it at this price point. The leather is soft, and the stitching is straight and tight. The tote has a reinforced bottom (without feet protectors), but it isn't a stiffly structured bag so it is sort of floppy when empty. It has a zippered top to secure the contents, and the interior is fully lined in a dark brown fabric that won't show ink stains or grime. The inside is basically two large open compartments divided by a zippered center compartment with a zippered compartment on the rear wall and two sleeves for cell phone or other gadget on the other wall. I don't think this small bag could fit a laptop, but I was able to fit a full size iPad along with books and some spiral notebooks inside it and still zip it shut. The opening of the center zippered compartment was a little too small to be able to get a full size iPad in though, but you could fit an iPad mini or 7" Kindle into it without a problem. The tote is well made with neatly done stitching, and zippers that open and close smoothly. The brass-tone hardware is lightweight and feels like plastic however, so while it's most likely adequate for everyday use, I'm not sure it could hold up to supporting a heavily loaded bag. All in all, this is a nice bag for the price, and it delivers the look of high end leather without the high price tag.

After using it for a while, I would say that's the best reason to purchase this. I can't say that I notice any improvement to either my health or the taste of my coffee from using this, but it looks great and it's easy to clean. I had a little trouble at first with the water taking overly long to drip through, but found that if I rolled the bottom of the filter a couple of times it worked fine. The first filters I used were some cheap off-brand, and I think that was the real problem. Since I've been using Melitta natural brown filters I don't think it's probably necessary to roll the bottom of the filter, but I've gotten used to doing it, and haven't tried it without. It's a nice piece of porcelain, and a fun way to make coffee.

product_title: Electrolux Icon E30GF74HPS Professional 30" Stainless Steel Gas Sealed Burner Range - Convection
clean_review_text: I needed a 30 inch free standing gas range. What I wanted was 4 burners (not 5), and an oven. That's it. No bells or whistles. Nothing extra. Just a simple design that was VERY WELL MADE..ACCURATE & PRECISE. I looked at all the high end names, and read all the reviews. The icon seemed to be the best product for the money. There were not allot of reviews for this particular item, and of the small number of reviews, some raved and some were dissatisfied. I was a bit stressed from the not so good reviews, but went ahead and bought this range. I am a person who loves to cook, and bake. I was hoping for precision, controlling the burners, and an oven that was consistent. I am happy to report that this stove delivered. I was prepared to have the gas air controls adjusted, (which would not be uncommon), but did not need to have anything adjusted. The combination of the size and power of the burners works well for me, and they all have the ability to be adjusted precisely. The oven is accurate and holds consistently. After reading so many opinions, I have to admit that I don't understand how people go to some discount store, and grab an appliance because the price is low, (especially an appliance of this caliber), and then expect miracles from it when there is no responsible customer-store relationship; no one who cares if the appliance was placed on the truck, and delivered with care, and respect; no one to call locally for help with any questions or problems that they may have. I purchased my stove from a reputable family appliance store._(for as good a price as I had seen anywhere), Although I was happy with it right from the start, the store encouraged me to call with any questions, and assured me that if I needed any adjustments, they would promptly send an experienced service person. This is a really nice unit for someone who enjoys cooking and appreciates simplicity._(I do not miss any of the useless electronics) When the oven is holding at 450 degrees, the stove top, and controls remain cool to the touch. This tells me that the oven is well insulated. The weight of the stove and solid feel of the controls tells me that the stove was put together with care and integrity. There is a learning curve with every new item. No two stoves or ovens will work exactly the same. Just go with it. That is how you become proficient. Yes I would recommend this stove to those who demand and appreciate the value of a well made tool. SIX MONTHS LATER: I am amazed at how pleased I am. I look forward to using it everyday. I love it's simplicity.. Not even a clock or timer. Just a great stove. (MADE IN USA, some parts possibly from Mexico)

clean_review_text: This is your standard world-class Hubbell plug. I am humbled by their design brilliance. Best feature: the backshell can be repositioned/rotated every 45 degrees. So no matter how the outlet is mounted, you can have the wire go in the direction you desire. Internally, Hubbell's standard sure-fire wire attachment system allows backwiring with assurance there will be no internal shorts or stray wire strands. Wires go into individual holes and segregate all conductors cleanly so nothing can short out. Wiring stripping gauge is molded into the plug, so you know exactly how to strip the wire. But there's also an 8-1/2x11, clearly-written instruction sheet with clear illustrations in a couple of languages, including English. Like I said, excellent engineering. It may be expensive but as the saying goes, quality is appreciated long after price is forgotten.

product_title: Amberistan Blue Amber Necklace for Women - HandMade from Baroque Amber Beads - plastic screw clasp, 18 inches - 46cm amber necklace for adults (Blue)
clean_review_text: I love the color of these beads and was excited to get them in. I thought amber came in only one color, so I did a Google search and discovered that there are different colors of amber, some rarer than others. One of the first things I did, when I received them, was try them on. They are beautiful. Like any good beads, the string is knotted between each bead. The connector is plastic, but that is so that when you secure the beads around your neck, the connection is hidden. Just be sure to screw in, not push in, the plastic connector. I measured the necklace. It is 18 inches or 44 cm long. The product heading says it is 17 inches long but this is typo, which needs to be corrected. I wanted to see if these were really amber, and not plastic beads. I decided not to use a hot needle to see if they would melt. Instead, I used the density test. I dissolved 1/8 cup of salt in 1 cup of warm water, until it was fully dissolved. I lowered the necklace into the solution. To my delight, the necklace floated. The beads are real amber. In conclusion, I am really happy with this item and hope to wear it often.

product_title: Dish Scraper, McoMce Durable Pan Scraper, Good Grip Kitchen Food Scraper, Multifunction Kitchen Scraper Used for Clears Leftover Food, Scraping Dough, Stir Etc, Silicone Sink Scraper, Easy to Clean
clean_review_text: I have spent years making homemade bread for my family and one of my most important tools has been a plastic dough scraper I got from a King Arthur seminar several years ago. Without explanation, my scraper simply disappeared without a trace and no one knows what happened to it (I think it got tossed in the trash by accident). I went about finding a replacement and after looking at a number of products I chose this one based on the price and the positive reviews. It has proven to be a good tool, a little different than my old scraper but still fine for my needs. Instead of plastic, this one is stainless steel encased in silicone. There are a couple of important factors here. First, because of the silicone shell the scraping edge is soft and pliable. This is helpful for scraping around crevices and getting as much dough from a round plastic bowl as possible. Because of the stainless steel inside, the whole is stiff though still slightly bendable which allows it to flex just enough to clean the bowl of my stand mixer without fatigue or permanently getting warped or bent. It is stiffer than my old plastic scraper, and while this is a positive thing it does require getting used to it. Unlike my old plastic scraper, this one will work on a hot saucepan. That’s good news for when I am making candy and don’t want to wait for the syrup to cool and harden, requiring a lot more effort to remove. I did not find any statement regarding the temperature limit for this scraper but based on other silicone utensils I have it is probably safe up to around 400 degrees F. And...silicone is more non-stick than plastic so clean-up is a little easier. It was never bad with with my plastic scraper, but it did require a bit more effort than this new scraper just because of the nature of the materials of each. I’m very pleased with this scraper. It meets the need I had and it is better than my old plastic scraper that I used for years. It works well, it can be used on hot or cold cookware and it is very easy to clean. It’s reasonably priced too. Based on my experience, this is a very easy product to recommend.
